events.description: There's no better way to spend a summer evening in Heber City than at the Saturday Sunset Music Series. The series features a diverse lineup of local musicians, highlighting the depth of talent in the Heber Valley and surrounding communities. events.title: Summer Festival at Wasatch Mountain State Park
events.description: Join us on Saturday, June 6th, from 11 am-2 pm at the visitor center for our Summer Festival. Summer brings fishing, hiking, biking, lawn games, and environmental educational activities for the whole family! Swoops, the Great Horned Owl, and Zephyr, the Red-tailed Hawk, will make an appearance June 6th is also National Trails Day and Free Fishing Day. It's a great day to be outside. If you want to get out early and join the Dig Day, meet at 9 am at the Phosphate Trailhead parking lot, earn your one-of-a-kind pin, and then enjoy the festival from 11-2 Try your skills at fishing at the pond. Free fishing day means you don't need a fishing license. And we aren't charging to use our poles! Worms are $3/dozen.
events.location: Wasatch Mountain State Park, 1281 Warm Springs Road

events.description: Readers ages 11 to 14 are invited to join “Tweens Who Read” at Wasatch County Library! This monthly book club usually meets on the second Wednesday of each month, with new summer hours from 4 to 5:30 p.m. Participants receive a free copy of the month’s featured book thanks to funding from the Wasatch Community Foundation. Meetings include snacks, discussion, and a fun activity inspired by the book. To sign up, visit the Wasatch County Library. For questions, email Brittnie at bhecht@wasatch.utah.gov. Learn more and see the full lineup of library events at Wasatch County Library.
events.location: Wasatch County Library, Heber City, UT

events.title: Wasatch History Speaker Series
events.description: Wasatch County Library invites the community to attend the next Wasatch History Speaker Series presentation on Wednesday, June 10, from 6–7 PM. History educators Dawain Wheatley and Easton Powell will explore the creation of the Declaration of Independence, the ideals that shaped the nation, and the document's continuing relevance as the United States approaches its 250th anniversary. The presentation will examine how the Declaration's principles have influenced communities throughout American history, including Wasatch County. This FREE event is part of the library's America250-inspired local history series, which commemorates 250 years of United States history, culture, and community stories. Seating is available on a first-come, first-served basis....
events.location: Wasatch County Library, Heber City, UT

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Wine Tasting & Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leisure. You'll ride in one of our beautiful vintage lounge cars. Tickets are sold either by the half table for 2 people (may be shared with 2 other guests), a full table for 4, and a limited number of single seats. Wines on the menu: Decoy Brut Cuvee Kumusha Sauvignon Blanc Cest La Vie Rouge (Pinot Noir & Syrah Blend) Larchago Tempranillo - Rioja, Spain Graham's Six Grapes Ruby Port

events.title: Longevity, Simplified: An Expert-Led Conversation
events.description: Black Rock Mountain Resort’s Onyx Club is partnering with local wellness experts for its June Speaker Series event, *Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health*, to focus on the practical application of long-term health optimization. The enlightening event, which is free and open to the community, will take place on Wednesday, June 24, from 5:30 to 7:00 p.m. at Black Rock Mountain Resort in Heber City, Utah. The event brings together two respected, local leaders in longevity, health, and performance: Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, co-founder of The Smart Fit Method. Designed to cut through the noise surrounding wellness trends and longevity advice, the discussion will focus on the four pillars of Longevity — sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress management — and how individuals can apply evidence-based strategies to improve daily performance, vitality, and long-term health outcomes. Participants are encouraged to attend with questions for the Q&A to follow the brief, informative presentations. Attendees will gain insight into: The lifestyle factors that have the greatest impact on longevity and overall health How advanced diagnostics such as lab testing, body composition analysis, and VO₂ max assessments can personalize wellness strategies Which health metrics matter most for optimizing performance and extending health span Practical ways to implement sustainable habits within demanding, real-world schedules Darnbrough will share the story behind The Smart Fit Method, a science-driven training system developed through his own experience overcoming severe injuries and rebuilding strength after a professional skiing career. The approach, embraced by everyone from elite athletes looking to boost their performance to seniors prioritizing their healthspan, emphasizes efficient, sustainable training designed to improve resilience, recovery, and long-term physical performance. events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Rigby Summer
events.description: Formerly touring as summerland, Oklahoma-based songwriter, Rigby Summer, weaves an Americana soundscape that reflects the road she’s traveled, from her Kansas City jazz upbringing to the beach pop of her former home in southern California to the red dirt roots of her current home in Stillwater, Oklahoma. While her influences range from Mary Chapin Carpenter to The Frames, Sia Furler to Johnny Cash, her sound often draws comparisons to Ingrid Michaelson and Peggy Lee—a little modern, a little old-fashioned, always earnest. In 2017, after a five-year hiatus from performing music, she founded the Monday@Modella songwriter pop-up and the ALL MUSIC STARTS LOCAL initiative and started sharpening her musical teeth, slowly carving a niche for herself in the Oklahoma scene. Since then, she has had the privilege of sharing the stage with and learning from a myriad of local and touring artists, including Bill Scorzari, Belle Plaine, Blake Berglund, Heather Styka and many more. She was also given honorable mention in the 2017 Woody Guthrie Festival Songwriting Contest and was a semi-finalist in the 2019 Wyoming Singer-Songwriter Contest, in which she was voted as the audience choice winner for her round. Hher first full-length album “Geography” was released in October 21 and broke the top ten on the NACC Folk Chart in November 2021....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Alicia Stockman
events.description: Alicia Stockman is a Utah-based folk-meets-Americana singer-songwriter whose music pulls back the veil to reveal everyday vulnerability. Her songs are written like intimate moments, drawing listeners into a relatable emotional journey, guided along by her whisky-dipped soulful vocals. Influenced by the vivid storytelling of powerhouse songwriters like Patty Griffin, Indigo Girls, and Jewel, she finds her home in the folk and Americana scene.
events.location: Heber City Park, Heber City, UT

events.title: Ragnar Trail
events.description: Ragnar Trail Soldier Hollow is happening August 28-29, 2026! Join us this summer for our return to the great state of Utah in our inaugural Solider Hollow trail relay! Run the trails at Soldier Hollow where Olympic history was made and future champions will compete. Smooth singletrack, rolling doubletrack, and a rugged ridgeline challenge your endurance while rewarding every step. Take in sweeping views of Heber Valley, Soldier Hollow Golf Course, Mount Timpanogos, and Deer Creek Reservoir by day, then experience the starry Wasatch skies by night! Register here: https://runragnar.com/pages/trail-solider-hollow...

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Thrive | FREE Youth Well-Being Skills Summer Program
events.description: Thrive is a FREE evidence-based program equipping Summit & Wasatch County students in grades 6-12 with vital life skills. events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
